ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #8, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Sep-14-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi, via ZOOM, for Barbara De Vries, Trustee.

The court begins by confirming that she has seen the Examiner's notes. Ms. Avid then offers oral testimony in support of her client's fee request. The court advises Ms. Alavi that it is likely to grant the fee requests with some modifications and instructs counsel to submit timesheets in the future that comply with local rules.

The Accounting is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Vedica Puri. Probate Examiner: Brandon Slykhous. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: F. Ruiz. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #7, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-18-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Avid Alavi for Barbara De Vries (Trustee).

Ms. Alavi stated that the Trustee is asking for a 1% guideline fee based on the carry value of the property at the end of the accounting period, that the property was initially valued at $730,000.00 and that when they last looked it is being valued at $1,202,969.00 which equates to a voluntary reduction of $9,459.00. Ms. Alavi also stated that the Trustee dealt with complex family dynamics, that there were a number of issues with threatening behavior from the Conservatee's Brother, that the Brother moved in and took over the bottom portion of the house and turned it into a bike repair shop, and that she had to deal with eviction. In addition, Ms. Alavi stated that the Trustee had to hire a bodyguard, a locksmith and a hazmat company, that she donated all of the bikes, that she accompanied the Caregiver and the Mother to get a restraining because they were fearful of the Brother, that the Mother was hospitalized at some point and that Ms. De Vries made multiple visits to check on the Mother. Furthermore, Ms. Alavi stated that the Trustee did trust administration, data entry, payroll, reconciled records, dealt with a Broker, did taxes, that she bears the sole responsibility and liability of this case, that she is self insured, that she has overhead, spoke of the cost of doing business in San Francisco, stated that $145.00 isn't enough for a Professional Fiduciary to cover expenses and make a living, that the rate has not been reviewed by the Court and it is not in line with the level of inflation, spoke of Ms. De Vries' level of education, experience, etc. The Seventh Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y. S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #7, REPORT, FEES

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar May-13-2019 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #6, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-12-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence S. Siracusa for petitioner Barbara DeVries.

The Court addressed concerns regarding the failure of the trustee to provide time sheets reflecting the time spent and work done, and that much of the work performed was in effect as a case manager rather than duties authorized to be performed as a trustee. Mr. Siracusa responded that the trustee went out of her way to do what was necessary to preserve the assets of the trust estate, and included the bringing of an unlawful detainer action to remove the son and obtaining a restraining order to protect Mr. Witherspoon and his property from injury by the son. He stated that Ms. DeVries had saved significant money by engaging a private attendant rather than hiring one through an agency. He added that she had voluntarily reduced her requested fee before making her fee request, and had saved the trust fromthe expense of having to engage a separate case manager. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Jaime. (Not reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-19-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for Barbara DeVries.

Ms. Alavi stated that the bonus given to the Caregiver is not considered a gift, that it's considered compensation, that it is typical to give caregivers holiday bonuses to encourage them to continue to do a good job and that it's an expense that is authorized under the special needs trust. In addition, Ms. Alavi stated that the annuity is part of the litigation with the Hopsital, that her office was not invloved in the litigation, that an Assistant of the Attorney that set up the annuity stated that there is no named Beneficiary and when no one is named that it automatically goes to the Estate. Ms. Alavi also stated that a petition for Substituted Judgment could be done. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
